data.body:
=== GATE DOGFOOD: ran the full criteria spectrum on the revised epic 5fd2 (default protocol) ===

Updated epic 5fd2 to fully reflect the grounded plan (core design bullets now carry exec-tier-aware execution +
exec/facet registry descriptor), then RAN THE GATE on it per our default protocol: DET floor (check_ac pass +
clarity 7); SINGLE-TURN tier on Opus, cached, facet-packed chunks of 6 (epic→size_factor 0.5×12=6) — ChunkA
[F1,E2,E6,F4,E3,G5], ChunkB [E1,E5,T5a,T5b,T5c,T5e] ×2 repeats + a broad open-ended pass; AGENT tier on Sonnet
with REAL Grep/Read/Glob over the rebar repo (E4,G1G2,A1). Writeup: docs/experiments/plan-review-gate-dogfood-baseline.md;
raw run: docs/experiments/data/gate_run.jsonl (+ run_gate.py).

FEEDBACK (high-signal, low-FP): FAILs were E4+G1G2 (agentic, caught the FALSE "solidlsp already in repo" claim —
glob over src/rebar/** finds NONE; solidlsp is a Serena-MCP dep, not a rebar library — a factual error only the
agentic tier could catch), G5 (epic with no children → decompose), E5 (no test ACs), E6 (no proving commands),
F1 ("measurably help" = post-sprint telemetry, not in-session), BROAD (no named invocation surface), T5a (no
claim-path latency target), T5c minor (signing integrity model unstated). PASS: F4,E1,E2,E3,A1,T5b,T5e.

REFINED THE EPIC from the findings + RE-RAN to check convergence: F1/E5/T5a/T5c FAIL→PASS; E6 major→minor (one
residual SC); G1G2 FAIL→AMBIGUOUS once the false claim was corrected; fixed the tooling claim ("NEW integrations,
solidlsp reachable via Serena MCP but not a rebar lib") + named the invocation surface (rebar review-plan /
run_workflow) + latency target + security-inheritance + a tests/verification AC (now 13 ACs, still passes
check_ac + clarity 7). G5 left FAIL INTENTIONALLY — correct: decomposition into the impl wave is the explicit
next step. Added a "Self-review note" section to the epic recording all this.

TUNING ASSESSMENT: NO major recalibration needed. Every FAIL was a real, addressable defect; every PASS correctly
silent; the revise loop converges; the agentic tier paid for itself (~$0.06/criterion). Two MINOR seams, both
already on the F-register: (1) E4 should split "contradicted" (block-worthy) from "unverified-pending-impl"
(advisory) → F3 confidence/severity floor; (2) G5 should gate container-decomposition criteria on a
ready-for-execution signal → proportionate-scrutiny routing. Baseline defaults (exec-tier split, facet chunking,
severity-ranked advisory, anti-FP) are well-calibrated.

NEXT: decompose 5fd2 into the one-wave implementation plan (child tasks w/ deps) — which also resolves the gate's
own G5 finding; file F1-F12 as discovered_from-linked tickets.
